best_extraction.authors: Devulder J, De Laat M, Van Bastelaere M, Rolly G
best_extraction.title: Spinal Cord Stimulation: A Valuable Treatment for Chronic Failed Back Surgery Patients
best_extraction.journal_name: Journal of Pain and Symptom Management
best_extraction.study_question: Evaluate long-term outcomes and cost-effectiveness of SCS in chronic failed back surgery syndrome.
best_extraction.population_assessed: 69 chronic failed back surgery syndrome patients treated with spinal cord stimulation.
best_extraction.follow_up_duration: 13-year study period; mean stimulator use 4.9 years
best_extraction.outcome_measures: Pain relief, return to work, drug intake, stimulator use, annual costs
best_extraction.inclusion_criteria: CFBSS, no surgical option, no psychological impairment, successful 2-week trial stimulation
best_extraction.answer_to_study_question: SCS gave sustained pain relief for many; 43 of 69 continued therapy with good relief.

reasoning: All models agreed on bibliographic fields and sample size. Case-series design was favored by two models and better fits an uncontrolled 69-patient follow-up. PubMed link came from one high-confidence model; DOI was unavailable.

field_level_decisions.study_design: Chose case_series_uncontrolled over retrospective_cohort_controlled due to two-model agreement and uncontrolled study description.
field_level_decisions.follow_up_duration: Merged consistent details: 13-year study window and mean 4.9-year device use.
field_level_decisions.doi: Returned null because other models indicated unavailable DOI with NAN.
field_level_decisions.pubmed_hyperlink: Accepted OpenRouter value because it was specific and high confidence.
